name: Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board
oneLine: edev.* dev environment exposed in CT; IIS ETag fingerprint leak
findings: edev.pngrb.gov.in subdomain in CT logs — internal/dev environment named publicly
IIS Server header restricted but ETag visible — partial fingerprint leak

fingerprints.stack: Microsoft IIS (Server header restricted) on Windows + likely .NET backend, GlobalSign OV TLS
versions: IIS version hidden but ETag visible
eolFlags: IIS version concealed; if Windows Server 2016, mainstream EOL Jan 2022

fingerprints.stack: Same IIS as parent; explicitly named development environment
eolFlags: dev subdomain in public CT — should not be externally indexed

attackPaths.name: Public edev subdomain → dev-environment configuration leak
attackPaths.entry: edev.pngrb.gov.in publicly registered in CT; typical dev exposes /web.config, /debug, test creds, /.env.example.
attackPaths.pivot: Harvest connection strings, API keys, test admin accounts; pivot from dev to prod via shared credentials.
attackPaths.objective: Compromise prod via dev path; exfiltrate dev DB which often mirrors prod schema/data.

attackPaths.name: IIS ETag fingerprint → version inference → CVE-targeted exploit
attackPaths.entry: ETag visible in HTTP responses leaks IIS version pattern.
attackPaths.pivot: Parse ETag → enumerate CVEs (CVE-2023-36434 HTTP/2 Rapid Reset; CVE-2023-21809 HTTP.sys memory corruption).
attackPaths.objective: RCE; SYSTEM-level access on Windows IIS host.

attackPaths.name: Soft-fail SPF + DMARC p=quarantine → email spoofing for regulator impersonation
attackPaths.pivot: Spoof PNGRB director-level emails to MoPNG/DGH/CMD audiences with urgent regulatory request.

paths.preAi: 3 hours (edev enumeration + endpoint discovery + config harvest)
paths.mythos: 30 min (subdomain fuzzing, common dev paths, parallel config-file extraction)

cisoPatchList.title: Remove edev.pngrb.gov.in from public DNS; migrate to internal-only
cisoPatchList.fix: Delete or NXDOMAIN the edev DNS record. Provision new dev on edev-internal.pngrb.local accessible only via VPN. Revoke public cert. Audit CT for any unauthorised edev certs issued.

cisoPatchList.title: Upgrade DMARC from p=quarantine to p=reject
cisoPatchList.fix: Monitor 30 days of rua reports for legitimate-sender failures; then change DMARC TXT to p=reject.

cisoPatchList.title: Confirm Windows + IIS patch level; apply current monthly cumulative updates
cisoPatchList.fix: Get-ItemProperty HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\InetStp for IIS version. wmic qfe list for KB inventory. Apply current Windows monthly cumulative.
cisoPatchList.validation: wmic qfe list shows current month's KB; Microsoft Update history clean

cisoPatchList.title: Suppress IIS ETag header or randomise to prevent fingerprinting
cisoPatchList.fix: web.config customHeaders: <add name='ETag' value='' /> or use opaque ETag pattern.
cisoPatchList.validation: curl -i pngrb.gov.in | grep -i etag returns empty or opaque

cisoPatchList.title: Audit .NET deserialization usage; replace BinaryFormatter with JSON
cisoPatchList.fix: grep -r BinaryFormatter | LosFormatter | NetDataContractSerializer in codebase. Migrate to System.Text.Json with strict type binding. ysoserial.net testing.

cisoPatchList.title: Implement CT monitoring for all PNGRB subdomains
cisoPatchList.fix: CAA records: 'CAA 0 issue "globalsign.com"'. Censys/SSLMate/certspotter monitoring on *.pngrb.gov.in.
cisoPatchList.validation: dig pngrb.gov.in CAA shows GlobalSign restriction; CT monitor active

phase2.headlineQuestion: Why does PNGRB maintain a publicly DNS-indexed development environment (edev.pngrb.gov.in), and what is the incident response if dev configuration data has been harvested?
